trans-unit.source: (content:references:wiki:plugins)=
trans-unit.target: (content:references:wiki:plugins)=

trans-unit.source: Plugins
trans-unit.target: Plugins

trans-unit.source: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__
trans-unit.target: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__

trans-unit.source: There are numerous extensions for QGIS, also called plugins, which provide extended functionalities. Usually, if you have a specific task and QGIS does not have the right functionality, look for a plugin. You google or search in the plugin window.
trans-unit.target: There are numerous extensions for QGIS, also called plugins, which provide extended functionalities. Usually, if you have a specific task and QGIS does not have the right functionality, look for a plugin. You google or search in the plugin window.

trans-unit.source: Plugins in QGIS are useful additional tools you can use to make some tasks easier. Plugins are written by QGIS developers and other independent users who want to extend the core functionality of the software. These plugins are made available in QGIS for all the users for free. Once installed, plugins remain available in QGIS.
trans-unit.target: Plugins in QGIS are useful additional tools you can use to make some tasks easier. Plugins are written by QGIS developers and other independent users who want to extend the core functionality of the software. These plugins are made available in QGIS for all the users for free. Once installed, plugins remain available in QGIS.

trans-unit.source: Installation of plugins
trans-unit.target: Installation of plugins

trans-unit.source: To install a plugin `Plugins` → `Manage and Install Plugins…` → `All` → Search for the plugin → `Install Plugin`.
trans-unit.target: To install a plugin `Plugins` → `Manage and Install Plugins…` → `All` → Search for the plugin → `Install Plugin`.

trans-unit.source: Manage Plugins
trans-unit.target: Manage Plugins

trans-unit.source: If you are currently not using installed plugins it might be useful to deactivate these plugins to avoid crowded toolbars and to reduce open panels.
trans-unit.target: If you are currently not using installed plugins it might be useful to deactivate these plugins to avoid crowded toolbars and to reduce open panels.

trans-unit.source: Allow experimental extensions
trans-unit.target: Allow experimental extensions

trans-unit.source: Experimental extensions are either still under development, or they are obsolete extensions that are no longer further optimised/adapted for the newer versions of QGIS. Nevertheless, the use of experimental extensions can be useful:
trans-unit.target: Experimental extensions are either still under development, or they are obsolete extensions that are no longer further optimised/adapted for the newer versions of QGIS. Nevertheless, the use of experimental extensions can be useful:

trans-unit.source: Specific functions are not supported in any other extension.
trans-unit.target: Specific functions are not supported in any other extension.

trans-unit.source: Alternative if there are problems with another extension.
trans-unit.target: Alternative if there are problems with another extension.

trans-unit.source: A tutorial makes use of a specific extension.
trans-unit.target: A tutorial makes use of a specific extension.

trans-unit.source: Downloading the Quick OSM plugin
trans-unit.target: Downloading the Quick OSM plugin

trans-unit.source: To download data from data and import it into your QGIS the plugin **QuickOSM** is great. First you need to install it by searching for it in the `Manage and Install Plugins` Tab.
trans-unit.target: To download data from data and import it into your QGIS the plugin **QuickOSM** is great. First you need to install it by searching for it in the `Manage and Install Plugins` Tab.

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} How to download the plugin
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} How to download the plugin

trans-unit.source: Manage and Install Plugins. :::
trans-unit.target: Manage and Install Plugins. :::

trans-unit.source: Installing QuickOSM. ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: Installing QuickOSM. ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: To launch the newly installed plugin, click on ![](fig/quickosmplugin.png) or click under `vector` → `QuickOSM`.
trans-unit.target: To launch the newly installed plugin, click on ![](fig/quickosmplugin.png) or click under `vector` → `QuickOSM`.

trans-unit.source: Follow the steps to fetch for data:
trans-unit.target: Follow the steps to fetch for data:

trans-unit.source: Select a Key and Value from the dropdown list. If you are unsure, check here: [taginfo.openstreetmap.org](https://taginfo.openstreetmap.org).
trans-unit.target: Select a Key and Value from the dropdown list. If you are unsure, check here: [taginfo.openstreetmap.org](https://taginfo.openstreetmap.org).

trans-unit.source: Choosing key and value in QuickOSM. :::
trans-unit.target: Choosing key and value in QuickOSM. :::

trans-unit.source: Limit the area by typing in the name of your area of interest.
trans-unit.target: Limit the area by typing in the name of your area of interest.

trans-unit.source: Unfold the tab `Advanced`. Only select the datatypes you are expecting to minimize errors.
trans-unit.target: Unfold the tab `Advanced`. Only select the datatypes you are expecting to minimize errors.

trans-unit.source: Running the QuickOSM plugin. :::
trans-unit.target: Running the QuickOSM plugin. :::

trans-unit.source: Click on `Run query`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Run query`.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} How to fetch data for multiple queries
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} How to fetch data for multiple queries

trans-unit.source: If you want to get more data in the same area, you can add a query by clicking on the ![](fig/plus_quickosm.png). Be careful choosing the right logical operator `And` or `Or`. If you are unsure check this [Wikipage](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_queries_wiki).
trans-unit.target: If you want to get more data in the same area, you can add a query by clicking on the ![](fig/plus_quickosm.png). Be careful choosing the right logical operator `And` or `Or`. If you are unsure check this [Wikipage](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_queries_wiki).
